EHL · Class of 2027

Analyst-in-training with a finance and strategy foundation, building toward roles in geopolitical risk, economic intelligence, and strategic advisory. My work sits at the intersection of quantitative modelling and open-source analytical tradecraft — building financial models and running regressions on one side, tracking conflict dynamics and verifying open-source intelligence on the other.

The outputs here are real deliverables: a 6/6 financial diagnostic on a NYSE-listed conglomerate, an econometric study on Italian equity market anomalies, a strategic paper on post-conflict reconstruction investment, and stakeholder intelligence operations for a sovereign investment forum with 10,000+ participants across 100+ countries.

01 —

Project Work

Corporate Finance · Equity Analysis Grade: 6 / 6
Deere & Company — Integrated Financial Diagnostic
Advanced Financial Analysis · EHL · 2024–2025 · NYSE: DE

Built a five-tab integrated Excel model covering income statement reconstruction, vertical and horizontal analysis, liquidity assessment, CAPM-based cost of capital estimation, and a full shareholder value creation framework. All source data verified against raw inputs; NOPAT margin methodology audited against the PAF source workbook. Awarded highest academic grade.

Coverage2020–2023 · NYSE: DE · Agri-industrial conglomerate
MethodsCAPM · WACC · NOPAT · TSR · Vertical / Horizontal Analysis
Output5-tab Excel model + PAF audit workbook (61/65 checks passed)
Selected findings from the model
Gross margin expanded from 33.4% (2020) to 38.4% (2023) — operating leverage confirmed across revenue acceleration of +72% over four years
Net margin nearly doubled from 7.7% to 16.6%; COGS as % of revenue fell 5 pp through disciplined cost management
NOPAT margin (EBIT × (1−t) / Revenue) rose from 10.3% to 19.7% — post-tax operating profitability more than doubled
Current ratio stable at 2.2–2.6× across all years; OCF exceeded $7.5B in three of four periods, peaking at $8.6B in 2023
ROE (38–48%) exceeded cost of equity Ke (7.1–17.0%) every year — value spread widening: +21pp → +30pp → +38pp
WACC 3.2–5.5%, beta ≈1.08; rising risk-free rate environment fully modelled across CAPM inputs 2021–2023

NOPAT Margin (PAF metric): EBIT × (1 − effective tax rate) / Revenue. Isolates post-tax operating profitability before financing costs. All values reconcile exactly to source inputs.

CAPM: Ke = Rf + β × ERP. WACC = Kd × Wd + Ke × We. Beta from market data; ERP from Damodaran premium approach.

Empirical Finance · Econometrics
The Monday Effect — FTSE MIB Anomaly Study
Corporate Finance 9501F · EHL · 2025–2026

Led the empirical section of a group study on calendar anomalies in Italian equity markets. Sourced 15 years of FTSE MIB daily return data from the LSEG Refinitiv terminal, cleaned holiday carry-forward errors, and ran OLS regression via Excel Analysis ToolPak with day-of-week dummy variables.

Dataset15 years · FTSE MIB · LSEG Refinitiv
MethodOLS Regression · Day-of-week dummies
FindingEffect attenuated to near-zero by 2020–2025
Healthcare Strategy · Consulting
NYC Health + Hospitals Elmhurst
EHL Strategy Challenge 2026 · Individual Contribution

Developed the Patient Communication & Data Confidentiality pillar for a strategic advisory submission to one of NYC's largest public hospital networks. Delivered SWOT analysis, three strategic recommendations with KPI frameworks, and an executive-level infographic.

PillarPatient Communication & Data Confidentiality
OutputSWOT · 3 recs · KPI framework · Infographic
Asset Management · Real Estate
Leonardo Royal Munich — LCR Strategy
Hotel Asset Management · EHL · May 2026

Lifecycle Capital Replacement strategy for a full-service upscale hotel in Munich under Invesco as landlord. Asset condition analysis, capex prioritisation, phased restoration scheduling, and Marriott Autograph rebrand alignment framework. Authored the financial modelling section within a six-person team.

FocusCapex planning · LCR scheduling · Operator alignment
RoleFinancial modelling owner · Team of 6
Real Estate Finance · DCF Modelling
Westin Las Vegas — OEREF Acquisition Analysis
Real Estate Finance · EHL · 2025–2026

Built the group's 10-year Excel DCF model for a hypothetical Open-End Real Estate Fund acquisition of the Westin Las Vegas. Three LTV scenarios (20/30/40%), interest-only debt structure, and unleveraged vs leveraged equity return analysis against a 4% minimum hurdle rate.

VehicleOEREF · Max LTV 40% · 4% hurdle rate
RoleExcel DCF model owner · Group of 6

"The work here spans financial modelling, econometric research, and geopolitical analysis — built to demonstrate that quantitative rigour and strategic intelligence are complementary, not separate disciplines."

03 —

Research

Geopolitical Risk · Investment Strategy · Reconstruction Economics
Hospitality Investment in Post-Conflict Reconstruction
Independent Research Paper · EHL Portfolio · 2026

Argues that Ukraine's reconstruction represents a time-limited first-mover opportunity for the hospitality sector — not in hotels, but in agri-food processing, cold chain logistics, and institutional catering. Draws on the Marshall Plan and Poland's post-Soviet food sector transition as historical analogues. Covers reconstruction financing mechanisms (EBRD, EU Facility, DFC), a structured risk matrix, and strategic entry sequencing for hospitality operators.

FocusAgri-food · Logistics · Institutional catering · Ukraine
FrameworkDestruction-reconstruction cycle · Financing vehicles · Risk matrix
SourcesWorld Bank · EBRD · FAO · WTTC · Control Risks · IMF
PostConflict_Hospitality_Hakanson.pdf · 10 pages
Open in new tab Download PDF
PDF preview not available on mobile — open in new tab ↗  or  download ↓
Open Source Intelligence · Ongoing
Conflict Monitoring & OSINT Analysis
Independent · 2022 – Present

Since 2022, independently tracking and fact-checking open-source reporting across multiple active conflict zones and geopolitical flashpoints. Methodology draws on the Bellingcat framework: reverse image search, geolocation, metadata extraction, Telegram channel analysis, and cross-source corroboration. Built through sustained engagement with primary sources, not passive consumption. This is the analytical foundation behind the reconstruction paper above.

Active monitoring since 2022
